name: Danny Bank
description: American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist and flautist (1922-2010)
abstract: Daniel Bernard Bank (July 17, 1922 – June 5, 2010) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist, and flautist. He is credited on some releases as Danny Banks.

concerts.label: Miles Davis with Gil Evans and Orchestra
Kool Jazz Festival: A Night of Genius-Artists Celebrate Mingus

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: That is the best saxophone section probably that Danny Bank ever played in. ... We had Al Cohn, wonderful band. ... Herbie Steward, Al Cohn, Frankie Socolow, who was playing alto by then I think.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: my sax section was already set — it was set for a while. Chuck Wilson and Jack Stooky on altos, Ken Peplowski and Doug Lawrence on tenors, and Danny Bank.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Schuller Gunther
about_quotes.evidence: The two saxophones, Danny Bank, and the flute player... They were about 15 feet away... Danny Bank is a half a bar separate from where we are playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Schuller Gunther — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
